phi_b_readiness.summary: Replay, event, and control-plane evidence already exists, but future doctrine must not promote convenience logs into semantic truth or bypass truth/perceived/render separation.
Checkpoint and snapshot substrate exists, but snapshots must remain lawful externalized support artifacts rather than hidden truth owners.
Safety and control surfaces show isolation pressure, but future doctrine must not overclaim guarantees not yet formalized.
Meaningful coexistence law depends on stronger versioning, release contract, archive, downgrade, and artifact identity doctrine from Υ.
Hotswap before stronger replay, snapshot, coexistence, and transaction/downgrade law would freeze unsafe continuity assumptions.
Authority-transfer and multi-node operational law remains high-risk until stronger replay, snapshot, provenance, and downgrade doctrine exists.
Live repo runtime substrate already exists and must be extended rather than replaced.

upsilon_readiness.summary: Preset, CI, and packaging surfaces already exist and should be frozen constitutionally.
Current preset and toolchain surfaces need convergence and authority clarity more than reinvention.
Release identity and policy substrate already exists and should be consolidated into explicit version doctrine.
Index and resolver surfaces already exist and should be aligned into one constitutional model.
Current release identity, naming, update, and policy surfaces are strong enough to support an explicit contract profile.
Naming rules already exist, but they should be tightened around versioning and release contract law.
Changelog surfaces exist and should be formalized instead of treated as self-justifying canon.
Archive and manifest engines already exist and should be formalized without becoming autonomous authority.
Existing update and rollback substrate already implies transaction continuity and can now be constitutionalized.
Current rollback and yanked-release surfaces are mature enough for doctrine, provided trust and publication remain gated.
Trust and publication surfaces are real, but safety policy correctly keeps them review-gated and non-casual.
The repo already contains release, update, trust, and control-plane substrate that should be consolidated rather than replaced.

zeta_blockers_or_prerequisites: sandboxing and isolation doctrine strong enough for privileged operational surfaces
stronger provenance and release-transaction continuity law
review-gated publication, trust, and licensing doctrine
stronger enforcement-oriented safety handling for privileged operational actions
ownership_projection_cautions: fields/ remains canonical semantic field substrate while field/ remains transitional
schema/ remains canonical semantic contract law while schemas/ remains a projection or validator-facing mirror
packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging and activation scope while data/packs/ remains scoped authored-pack authority
canonical prose and semantic specs outrank projected or generated mirrors
the thin runtime/ root is not canonical by name alone
older planning numbering drift remains evidence rather than authority

final_verdict.reason: Both Φ-B and Υ are now the correct next two series, but they must be interleaved and partially gated rather than treated as uniformly ready bands.
modifications: Run early Φ-B continuity doctrine before late-gated runtime authority topics.
Run core Υ versioning, release-contract, archive, transaction, and downgrade work before multi-version coexistence and hotswap review.
Keep publication, trust, licensing, and other externally consequential work review-gated.
Keep Ζ dependency-shadowed until listed runtime and release/control-plane prerequisites are explicit.
